resources.string: Please start Screen Off Run service first
This software does not collect, record, or upload any of your information.
Tap to activate Screen Off Run

Screen Off Run requires ADB or Root permission to activate.
ADB method: Enable USB Debugging on your phone, connect to a computer, and run the following command in a terminal/cmd window:
adb shell %s

For devices with Android 6.0 and above, you can also use Shizuku to activate (Shizuku activation is essentially the same as ADB permission activation).
Screen Off Run service activation required
ADB command copied to clipboard: adb shell %s

You can tap the "Screen Off Run" title on the main interface at any time to open this help screen.


While Screen Off Run is active, all apps will continue to work just as they do when the screen is on. You don't need to worry about the system automatically sleeping after a period of inactivity; as long as you enable the Global Control service and Floating Ball, the system will not sleep automatically unless you manually press the power button.


When the "Raise to Wake" feature is enabled, the app monitors device orientation changes after the screen is off. This consumes a very small, almost negligible amount of battery.


Note: Most devices block touch input when the screen is off via this method, so double-tap to wake only works on a few devices (such as Samsung S-Pen tablets, iQOO 9, Redmi Note 1 LTE, etc.).


A few devices block hardware keys after the screen is off, so using volume keys to wake won't work on those devices (e.g., some Huawei HarmonyOS or Origin OS phones). Users with these devices can contact me to research using the getevent command to detect volume keys, which might be a viable workaround.


Some LCD devices still have a backlight active after the screen is turned off this way. Similar to blocking keys or touch, this is determined by system engineers and cannot be changed by the app. However, you can minimize the backlight by lowering the screen brightness to the minimum before using Screen Off Run.
